excludedTaskOwners.T569: frontend test reduction and workflow optimization

methodology.sourceSnapshot: accepted base plus this audit's two documentation artifacts only
methodology.lloc: Nonblank source lines after TypeScript comment-token spans or CSS block comments are removed; strings and regular expressions are preserved.
methodology.component: A PascalCase function, variable, or class declaration with a JSX return path.
methodology.hook: A declared custom hook whose name starts with use; the Zustand store hook is counted as a store, not a hook.
methodology.apiClientModule: One of the 31 endpoint modules that imports the central Axios owner, plus api/index.ts.
methodology.page: A production file under pages whose basename ends in Page.tsx.
methodology.layout: A JSX component symbol declared under components/layout.
methodology.reachabilityRule: Resolve static imports, re-exports, dynamic imports, and side-effect imports by TypeScript module-resolution rules. Tests are included only when classifying production exports as runtime-used, test-only, or unreferenced.
methodology.unusedExportRule: Classify by TypeScript symbol identity across production and tests; do not aggregate solely by text name. Reachable route-default exports are excluded from the dead set.

featureClassifier: admin-ops: pages and API modules whose names start with Admin or market provider operations
auth-access: auth pages, access guards, auth context, auth hooks, and auth API
backtest: backtest pages, components, API, and types
home-analysis: home pages/components plus analysis task ownership and stockPool store
market-research: market overview, cockpit, rotation, liquidity, options/scenario market evidence, and related API owners
stock-research: stock structure and stock report surfaces
watchlist-research-queue: watchlist and research queue pages/components/API/types
shared-shell-platform: every remaining root, common/layout/terminal/linear, context, hook, i18n, and utility owner

routes.finding: All production routes are registered and all route targets are reachable. The locale and non-locale trees duplicate declarations, not behavior. The 24 alias identities lack surviving in-repo callers, but history shows recent restoration and no external bookmark or telemetry evidence exists, so they are not classified as dead.
routes.breadcrumbFinding: No production Breadcrumb symbol or breadcrumb-specific owner was found; current route context is expressed through navigation resolution, document title, and page headings.

deadAndOrphanAnalysis.classificationCaveat: A same-named DuplicateTaskError runtime class in api/analysis.ts is used. Only the independently declared interface in types/analysis.ts is unreferenced; this is why symbol identity, not name-only matching, is required.

legitimateRepeatedEndpointBranches: api/marketRotation.ts: GET /rotation-radar with and without market parameters
api/stocks.ts: POST parse-import with file and text bodies
apiOwnership.fragmentationConclusion: REST transport is not fragmented. Market response types, readiness/freshness/source projection, and page-level market view models are fragmented across api/market.ts, api/marketOverview.ts, api/marketDecisionCockpit.ts, api/marketRotation.ts, api/liquidityMonitor.ts, api/optionsLab.ts, api/scenarioLab.ts, and page/component projection helpers.
cacheOwners: api/index.ts: short-lived GET cache and in-flight map
stores/stockPoolStore.ts: stock pool state and request sequencing
browser preference and session owners: scoped persisted UI state

conclusions: PortfolioPage has the highest raw local state count.
HomeBentoDashboardPage has the highest raw effect count.
UserScannerPage has the highest overall hook and memoization count.
The backtest family has the strongest duplicated state/prop ownership across normal, pro, historical, result, audit, and compare surfaces.
OptionsLab has four request-key/effect chains with local cancellation booleans and is a bounded controller/reducer candidate.
Module-level request maps, modal stacks, product-surface external state, i18n state, and stockPool request sequencing have explicit owners and are not dead globals.

componentComplexity.assessment: overloaded: accounts, cash, holdings, trades, import, drawers, and risk
overloaded: configuration sections and legacy projection
overloaded: highest hook count and dependency fan-out
overloaded: watchlist, queue, research overlays, and alerts
large render branching but cohesive admin-log domain
result normalization and view ownership are mixed; branch counters were not retained in the evidence snapshot
excessive prop contract and render responsibility; memo counter was not retained in the evidence snapshot
large but cohesive shell and accessibility owner; branch counters were not retained in the evidence snapshot

largeCohesiveExceptions: MarketProviderOperationsPage.tsx is 4,386 LLOC but its main component is about 450 physical lines; panel composition, not a monolithic component, drives file size.

MarketOverviewWorkbench.tsx is 3,558 LLOC but its main component is about 126 physical lines; transformation and registry code drives file size. Its risk is semantic authority, not raw component state.
Shell.tsx is large but owns navigation, keyboard, focus, responsive rail, and session shell behavior coherently.

substantiveCrossFileExactGroups: StressScenarioDetail in BacktestAuditTables and DeterministicBacktestResultPage: 7 exact fields
ScenarioRunState in BacktestAuditTables and DeterministicBacktestResultPage: 7 exact fields
StockStructureDecisionConsumerIssue and PortfolioStructureReviewConsumerIssue: 4 exact fields; likely domain-local, verify before consolidation
HomeDailyChangeView and StockResearchFact: 4 exact fields; likely coincidental view shape, retain unless semantics align
nearDuplicateAuthorities: MarketSnapshotPayload (api/market.ts) versus MarketDataMeta (api/marketOverview.ts): 45 shared of 51/45 fields, Jaccard 0.882
DeterministicBacktestMetrics versus RuleBacktestCompareRunMetrics: 16 shared of 17/16 fields, Jaccard 0.941
DataSourceAcquisitionPriorityQueueItem versus DataAcquisitionActionPackItem: 13 shared of 13/14 fields, Jaccard 0.929
ScannerCandidateResearchSummaryFrame component model versus types/scanner model: 13 shared of 13/15 fields, Jaccard 0.867
MarketRegimeSynthesisEvidenceItem versus LiquidityImpulseSynthesisEvidenceItem: 17 shared of 18/19 fields, Jaccard 0.850
GateCopy versus AccessGatePageProps: 8 shared of 8/9 fields, Jaccard 0.889
typeAndSchemaAnalysis.conclusion: The primary parallel type authority is the market snapshot/metadata family. Backtest result metrics and research handoff targets are the next bounded consolidation candidates. Optionality and unknown values cannot be tightened mechanically because they frequently encode unavailable or versioned contract state.

primitiveFanIn.assessment: too generic and high-conflict; split visual atoms by surface/control/status/layout without a compatibility barrel
mixes layout, data rows, and disclosures; split after feature migrations
active migration residue; flatten only after preserving responsive behavior

sharedComponentsToRetain: StatusBadge visual layer with domain-specific projection outside the primitive
accessibilityOwners: Drawer centralizes modal stack, body lock, focus trap, Escape, focus restoration, and inert behavior.
ConfirmDialog reuses the common modal overlay contract.
AuthGuardOverlay remains specialized because it is a fail-closed authentication gate.
Menu-level Escape handling in scanner, watchlist, sidebar, shell, and charts is interaction-specific and is not duplicate modal ownership.
designAccessibilityResponsive.responsiveFinding: Responsive ownership is split between useIsDesktopViewport, direct page media/layout rules, and shared primitives. Consolidation needs mobile drawer, table overflow, and focus verification; CSS similarity alone is not deletion evidence.
mobileDesktopRenderFamilies: components/layout/Shell.tsx: desktop navigation versus mobile Drawer; retain separate interaction semantics and shared route label owner
components/backtest/ProBacktestWorkspace.tsx: desktop and mobile execution rails; share projection, retain responsive DOM where reading order differs
pages/OptionsLabPage.tsx: mobile contract cards versus desktop table; share row view model, retain appropriate semantics
pages/LiquidityMonitorPage.tsx: mobile indicator cards versus desktop dense view; share evidence projection
pages/AdminUsersPage.tsx: mobile holding cards versus desktop holdings table; share row projection
pages/AdminLogsPage.tsx: mobile business-event cards versus desktop table; share event projection
pages/PortfolioPage.tsx: mobile holding cards versus desktop table; share account-scoped row projection
components/scanner/ScannerCandidatePresenters.tsx: mobile candidate rows versus dense presentation; share candidate truth model
pages/DeterministicBacktestResultPage.tsx: mobile digest supplements rather than duplicates the full report and should be retained
designAccessibilityResponsive.noOpCompatibility: Button.glow is declared, destructured, intentionally ignored, and has one production caller. Four LLOC can be removed with no intended visual behavior change.

confirmedObsolete: components/common/Button.tsx glow prop and its single LLMChannelEditor caller
activeFeatureFlags: VITE_WOLFYSTOCK_ADMIN_MISSION_CONTROL_PROTOTYPE_ENABLED is resolved by utils/adminCapabilities.ts and keeps the bounded admin prototype fail-closed; it is not obsolete
activeCompatibilityToRetain: stockPool deprecated history keys and purge path
Settings legacy LLM keys and route projections
marketOverview legacy freshness normalization until one truth owner replaces it atomically
insufficientEvidence: 24 route alias identities have no in-repo callers but lack bookmark, telemetry, and support-window evidence.
index.css is large and initial, but no CSS coverage artifact proves which selectors are unused.
Module-internal-only exported symbols are visibility debt, not production-code deletion evidence.

truthReview.finding: Static inspection did not prove any protected truth collapse. The material risk is parallel projection logic drifting across market, readiness, evidence, and page view-model owners. Development fixtures are explicitly DEV/TEST separated and are not production fallbacks.

bundleAndLoading.command: ./wolfy exec --profile test -- npm --prefix apps/dsa-web run build:bundle -- --outDir $TMP_BUILD_DIR
findings: Only vendor-react, vendor-router, and index CSS are modulepreloaded from the entry document.
Admin and provider-diagnostics chunks remain separate and are not initial modulepreloads.
UserScanner and Settings nested lazy boundaries are deliberate product-surface gates and should be retained.
Dead exports are expected to have near-zero shipped-bundle impact because Vite tree shaking already removes most unreachable code.
The global CSS asset is the strongest possible initial-byte candidate, but selector coverage is required before any deletion estimate becomes actionable.

historicalEvidence: Legacy stock redirects were introduced or restored in June 2026; they are too recent to delete without telemetry.
Rough shell helpers were still used by migrations through July 2026; they are migration residue, not dead code.
Button.glow dates to February 2026 and is explicitly ignored after deprecation while one caller remains.
Scanner and Settings nested lazy boundaries were deliberately introduced in commits e86f3eb5 and 8e12bb23.
No production TODO or FIXME residue was found by the audit scan.

includes: 23 fully unreferenced symbols plus two dead barrel re-export lines: 201 LLOC
ignored Button.glow declaration, plumbing, and caller: 4 LLOC
excludes: test-only production exports owned jointly with T569
route aliases without external usage evidence

moderate.basis: Nonoverlapping low/high estimates for route declaration consolidation; backtest, scanner, home, options, portfolio, watchlist, market truth, type, design primitive, rough-shell, and settings ownership lanes. These are implementation targets, not proven deletions.

gates: CSS coverage before an estimated 600-1200 LLOC style reduction
route telemetry and a published support window before an estimated 30-60 LLOC alias reduction
managed-browser evidence before design-system or route architecture changes
contract-owner agreement before market or persisted compatibility removal
aggressive.recommendation: Do not schedule as one restructuring task. Execute the roadmap lanes and remeasure after each owner-boundary change.

maximumSafeParallelPlan.reason: route/access owner is shared and protected
disjoint feature owner sets; serialize tests/builds and coordinate T569/T575
shared market truth and type authority

roadmap.resourceRule: Vitest, build, Playwright, and managed-browser qualification are serialized and scheduled away from T566's full-suite resource window.

directConclusions.highConfidenceRemovableProductionLoc: 205 exact LLOC (0.125%): 201 unused export/re-export LLOC plus 4 ignored Button.glow LLOC.
directConclusions.mostDuplicatedComponents: Backtest. Market-research has the highest raw component count (112), but the backtest family has the strongest repeated workspace, result, audit-table, metric, and prop-contract structures.
directConclusions.mostDuplicatedStateOrEffects: Backtest has the strongest duplicated state ownership across modes and results. Raw single-file maxima differ: PortfolioPage has 66 state/reducer calls, HomeBentoDashboardPage has 21 effects, and UserScannerPage has 140 total hooks.
directConclusions.fragmentedApiOwnership: Transport is not fragmented: api/index.ts is the sole Axios owner and there is no raw fetch. Market semantic response/type/truth projection is fragmented across market endpoint and page/helper owners.
directConclusions.obsoleteCompatibilityPaths: Only Button.glow is proven obsolete. Route aliases and persisted legacy keys require telemetry/version support evidence; existing fixture and migration paths are active and must not be deleted as fallbacks.
directConclusions.parallelTypeAuthorities: MarketSnapshotPayload/MarketDataMeta is the strongest parallel authority; backtest metrics/result state, research handoff targets, settings acquisition queue items, and scanner summary frames are bounded secondary candidates.
directConclusions.tooGenericComponents: TerminalPrimitives and LinearPrimitives are high-fan-in mixed-responsibility modules; rough shell wrappers are migration residue. They should be split by visual responsibility, not expanded into domain-semantic super-components.
directConclusions.componentsToShare: Drawer, ConfirmDialog, ApiErrorAlert, visual-only status/empty/notice primitives, Pagination, and reduced-motion ownership.
directConclusions.sharedComponentsToSplit: TerminalPrimitives and LinearPrimitives after feature migrations; split into direct-import surface/control/status/layout/feedback atoms with no compatibility barrel.
directConclusions.independentPageFamilies: Backtest, scanner, home, settings, options, portfolio, and watchlist/research can migrate on disjoint owners. Route authority, market truth, design primitives, and global CSS remain serial lanes.
directConclusions.safestFirstWave: T600: delete only the 23 verified unused symbols, two dead barrel lines, and Button.glow no-op path, with targeted tests and a typecheck.
directConclusions.maximumSafeParallelPlan: After serial T600 and T601, run at most four owner-disjoint lanes in parallel: T602 backtest, T603 scanner, T604 home, and T607 settings. Then at most three: T605 options, T608 portfolio, T609 watchlist/research. Serialize T606 market truth, T610 primitives, T611 CSS, and all heavy validation.

residualUncertainties: Static reachability cannot observe external bookmarks, embedded links, runtime plugin lookup, or support contracts; route aliases are not deletion candidates without telemetry.
Unused-export analysis is a source snapshot and must be rerun at each implementation base, especially after T569.
Component branch and logical-body metrics are syntax-derived prioritization signals, not semantic complexity proofs.
The audit did not execute browser, Playwright, UAT, QoderWork, backend tests, full gate, or release-real-runtime, by task contract.
Bundle bytes are one production build snapshot; gzip values use local level 9 except where Vite's rounded chunk report is explicitly labeled.
No CSS selector coverage exists, so global style deletion remains unquantified.
No backend contract changes were audited; T606 and T607 require coordination with T575.
No protected truth collapse was proven by static inspection; parallel authority is a drift risk, not evidence of an existing user-visible lie.

validation.arithmeticAssertions: passed: owner/feature totals, route/large-file counts, dead-export sums, bundle initial bytes, scenario totals, candidate/roadmap uniqueness and required fields
validation.gitDiffCheck: passed: exactly two authorized added files and no whitespace errors
validation.changedFileSecretPrivatePathScan: passed: no absolute user-directory, private-key header, credentialed URL, or high-confidence token pattern
